meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop consuming tutorials and start shipping. Learn a founder-focused project management framework using Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable SaaS.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a symptom of passive consumption, not lack of ability. By shifting to a 'build-first' project management framework, you treat every feature as a revenue-generating asset. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to automate implementation, forcing your brain to solve real-world problems instead of following scripts.
content.intro:
You have dozens of half-finished repositories gathering digital dust. You’ve watched the videos, read the docs, and mastered the theory, yet your bank account remains stagnant. Tutorial hell isn't a knowledge gap; it's a focus trap that keeps you in a state of perpetual preparation while the market moves on without you.

To break the cycle, you must stop being a student and start being an architect. We are replacing passive learning with a rigorous project management workflow designed for founders. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, we turn your product roadmap into an execution engine, ensuring every line of code you write directly contributes to your MVP’s market viability.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-Generating Core
steps.description: Identify the single feature that solves a painful problem for a paying user. Strip away all 'nice-to-haves' to focus on the core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this problem statement: [Insert Problem]. Create a lean technical requirements document for a SaaS MVP focusing on the critical path to user payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Create a project roadmap file that outlines the MVP feature set based on the Antigravity requirements document.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Don't write code until you define the data. A robust schema prevents technical debt and ensures your MVP can scale with user growth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a normalized SQL schema for a SaaS platform managing users, subscriptions, and core entity data. Optimize for Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Apply the generated schema to my local database and create the necessary migration files.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Use automated tools to build your boilerplate. Avoid manual setup; your time is better spent on business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a Next.js app with Auth, Tailwind, and Supabase integration configured for rapid deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Scaffold the project structure based on the Antigravity boilerplate and install all necessary dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Feature by Feature
steps.description: Work in 90-minute blocks. Use Claude Code to generate the implementation while you act as the system architect.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the core feature into 3 distinct functional tasks. Write pseudo-code for the backend logic of the primary user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Implement the first functional task from the Antigravity breakdown and write unit tests for the critical path.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship early. Your goal is to get the product in front of a real human being to confirm they will pay for it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment checklist for a production-ready SaaS build, including essential monitoring and error tracking configurations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Configure GitHub Actions for automated deployment to Vercel and verify the environment variables.'

pro_tips: Never start with code; always start with a user story that ends in a credit card transaction.
Treat your IDE like a terminal; use Claude Code to offload the syntax heavy-lifting so you can focus on system flow.
If you spend more than 2 hours debugging, delete the code and rethink the architecture; complexity is the enemy of profit.
Use Antigravity to generate your 'How to test' documentation before you write the actual implementation.
Launch your MVP as soon as the core feature works; features are meant to be added post-launch, not pre-launch.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, spend 80% of your time on customer acquisition and 20% on iterating based on actual user feedback, not your own assumptions.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building toys and start building businesses. The market doesn't care about your code quality as much as it cares about your value delivery. Stop watching, start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
